species: Candidatus Accumulibacter (proximus BATAC285-related MAG; nuwaii SCUT-4 recovered from same reactor)

growth_records.strain: SBR1 glucose enrichment (BATAC285-related MAG 5.7%; genus-level Ca. Accumulibacter 9.7%)
growth_records.medium: EBPR sequencing batch reactor (SBR1), glucose-fed synthetic wastewater

up.units: mg TOC/L (batch-test amount consumed, NOT a per-time rate)

sec.units: mg/L (batch-test amount released, NOT a per-time rate)

growth_records.cite: Xiaojing Xie et al. Metagenomic characterization of the metabolism, evolution, and global distribution of Candidatus Accumulibacter members in wastewater treatment plants. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf278. [PMC12766714]
growth_records.snippet: we enriched PAOs using glucose as the carbon source for 190 days... the relative abundance of a MAG closely related to Ca. Accumulibacter proximus BATAC285 reaching 5.7%... Batch test with glucose as a carbon source showed highly linked P r
growth_records.notes: Enrichment co-culture; Ca. Accumulibacter only 9.7% genus-level (BATAC285-related MAG 5.7%), so measured values reflect the mixed community not a pure isolate. Reported glucose uptake (20.36 mg TOC/L) and P release (8.0 mg/L) are AMOUNTS in a batch test, not per-time or per-biomass rates; cannot derive mmol/gDW/h without biomass and time (not given). Established to resolve that GEMs predicted glucose use but PTS transporter ptsG absent and no glucose use in short-term batch experiments; long-term (190 d) enrichment did show glucose-linked P release. Values quoted directly; no arithmetic performed.
